summary: Migrated Study child pages to canonical Study record ID routing, using /pages/study/<area>/?id=<study-record-id>, with /pages/study/synthesis/ replacing the legacy /pages/synthesize/ surface.

task.request: Move Study child routes away from pid/sid URLs. Consent forms, guides, participant consent, participants and synthesis should load from the Study record ID only. Synthesis should live under /pages/study/synthesis/.

policyClarification.userNote: Policy intent is to block tool access until explicit consent or permission gates are satisfied, not to remove API tool access entirely.
policyClarification.implementationImpact: The change preserves tool-capable code paths but keeps them behind explicit route context, authentication, permission and consent gates. It does not add blanket removal of API access.

decisions.decision: Use ?id=<Study record id> for Study child pages.
decisions.reason: A Study already carries the linked Project record ID. Requiring pid on every child page duplicates state and creates scope for mismatched URLs.

decisions.decision: Keep legacy pid/sid support inside the resolver only where the Study record resolves and confirms the Project link.
decisions.reason: A legacy route is only useful if it proves the same record relationship as the canonical route. Otherwise it should fail closed.

decisions.decision: Use route-loader modules for child pages.
decisions.reason: Several existing controllers still expect legacy pid/sid parameters during boot. A top-level loader ensures the canonical bridge resolves first, exposes the canonical context, and only then imports the legacy-compatible controller.

decisions.decision: Remove the pages.dev production API fallback from shared Study route context.
decisions.reason: Preview pages must call their same-origin /api/* proxy so the branch preview Worker and D1 preview seed are used. Falling through to the production Worker can reproduce stale or unauthorised behaviour.

decisions.decision: Move synthesis from /pages/synthesize/ to /pages/study/synthesis/ and keep the old page as a redirect.
decisions.reason: Synthesis is study-scoped. The route should communicate that scope and use the same canonical Study record ID pattern as the other Study child pages.

decisions.decision: Pin the routing contract with route-state tests rather than relying on manual review.
decisions.reason: The regression pattern has been stale imports, incorrect query parameters and branch preview/API divergence. These are precisely the kind of problems route-state tests can catch.

changes: Created Study child route loaders so the canonical Study URL bridge runs before consent forms, guides, participant consent, participants and synthesis controllers.
Updated consent forms page to load through /js/consent-forms-route-loader.js with a cache-busted study-record-id-routing query.
Updated guides page to load through /js/guides-route-loader.js instead of importing the context and page controller directly.
Updated participant consent page to load through /js/participant-consent-route-loader.js and changed its missing-context copy to require a Study record ID rather than a project ID and study ID pair.
Updated study participants page to load through /js/participants-route-loader.js.
Updated Study synthesis page to load through /js/synthesis-route-loader.js and removed its direct synthesize-page import.
Created a route-state test for Study child routing to pin canonical id URLs, loader ordering, synthesis route migration and same-origin preview API behaviour.
Updated shared Study route context to keep API calls same-origin unless an explicit API origin is configured.

validation.workflowStateObservedAfterPush: CI, Worker CI, Validate ResearchOps, Release Gate, Format pull request, QA Broken links, Accessibility audit and qa-bdd were in progress when checked.

residualRisks: Some child page controllers still contain legacy compatibility code. The route loaders ensure canonical context is resolved first, but a later cleanup should remove pid/sid dependencies after preview validation.
The session runner route still uses pid/sid parameters. It was kept out of this change because the user's requested set was consent forms, guides, participant consent, participants and synthesis.
Airtable remains rate-limited in preview, so child pages that depend on Airtable-backed materials may show empty or error states unless D1 coverage exists for that table.
The legacy /pages/synthesize/ redirect can only fully canonicalise when the Study record resolves. If the old URL cannot resolve, it falls back to id=sid as a best-effort user escape hatch.
nextValidationTargets: If any check fails, inspect job logs and fix the exact failing assertion or formatting issue.
Open the five Study child routes on the branch preview with id=rect3o7dt and confirm each page calls /api/* on the preview host, not the production Worker.
Open /pages/synthesize/ with legacy pid/sid and confirm it redirects to /pages/study/synthesis/?id=rect3o7dt.
Confirm the Study landing page task cards point to canonical child routes with id only.
